extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won sprint after fighting for ASGREEN Kasper's wheel in final 2km, then punching clear on 12% cobbled ramp finish.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN physically battled DEHGENCOL for ASGREEN's leadout wheel in final 2km, showing aggression typically avoided by pure sprinters.
Trek-Segafredo controlled final 2km despite SAGAN's team, suggesting Bora accepted parasitic tactics when leadout matched their needs.
12% cobbled ramp finale in final 200m punished pure sprinters, rewarding SAGAN's puncheur characteristics over flatland speed.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: two bike changes inside 20km yet still won, indicating mechanical resilience didn't compromise positioning or confidence
rider_signals.BODNAR Maciej: positive: paced SAGAN back after second bike change late in stage, demonstrating Bora's domestique depth in flat stages
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: finished third after SAGAN punched clear on steep ramp, confirming inability to match power-sprinters on gradient finishes
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: neutral: provided leadout that both SAGAN and DEHGENCOL fought to follow, validating Trek's leadout train quality
